title: It's not possible to send a large XLS file using a stream writter
author: bushev

comments.author: Sjonnie2nd
comments.body:
Hi Bushev,

Does it work when you commit (=send) each row individually?:
`worksheet.addRow(data).commit();`

comments.author: bushev
comments.body:
@Sjonnie2nd Hi!

Yes, I have tried. Unfortunately with the same result..

comments.author: bushev
comments.body:
I updated code example, now it not requires mongo collection, just copy & run.

Result is the same:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/7535766/19804622/1efa3a14-9d18-11e6-8d47-cc668bf95c9c.png)

Then:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/7535766/19804656/50fbafa2-9d18-11e6-8bad-4a450a32bf92.png)

comments.author: Sjonnie2nd
comments.body:
First a view tips.
- I see you removed the `worksheet.commit()`. It will still be needed at the end.
- In my usages of the writer I've put a `response.end()` at the end. Don't know if it's required but it doesn't bring me an errror.
- In my usages it works without `'Transfer-Encoding': 'chunked'`.

But all of this is not the cause of the problem.

I've tested your code on Window 10 with Chrome, Edge and IE. All 3 of them cancel the download at exactly 2 minutes. First I thought it's a clientside problem 

But wait! Node's http server has a timeout of 120000. When I set it to 300000 (5 min.) and test it with 240 ticks x 1000 (4 min.) it works! So..: 
`}).listen(2000).timeout = 300000;`

comments.author: bushev
comments.body:
The same after applying your comments.

Yes, **it will work if I increase a timeout and I must do that.**

But, the end user will see "Starting - 0 B/s - 0 B" about 5 minutes, and nothing else (no progress or how much bytes already downloaded at least), this will confuse the user..

I suspect this library "cache" all data and flush it to the user at the end only. I have tested it with `wget`, it also shows 0 bytes written. What do you think about it?

comments.author: Sjonnie2nd
comments.body: Looking at worksheet-writer.js it looks like row.commit() really tries to write the row directly to the response. Also Chrome looked to show an animated response on every row/second but perhaps that was accidentally. I've no idea. Anyone else?

comments.author: wwwouter
comments.body:
I tried all the headers from this post https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6258210/how-can-i-output-data-before-i-end-the-response and still it stalls for me as well.

These are the headers that I'm using right now.
```
{
'Transfer-Encoding': 'chunked',
'Connection': 'Transfer-Encoding',
'X-Content-Type-Options': 'nosniff',
}
```

comments.author: serkandurusoy
comments.body:
Did you guys figure this out? In my case as well, download does not begin and only after the file is fully generated the download happens in an instant. So it is almost like the streaming interface does not stream ;) 

I do have commit() sprinkled on every possible place as well.

comments.author: bushev
comments.body: Hi guys! My workaround (using a temporary file) here: https://github.com/bushev/node-lib/blob/master/lib/controllers/crudcontroller.js#L1354

comments.author: GibreelAbdullah
comments.body: Is this issue resoled? Without the workaround of a temp file, I mean?

comments.author: ramesh-joshi
comments.body: Hi @Siemienik, Is this issue resolved? Without the workaround of a temp file?

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body: Actually no. However, the author of this issue resolves this issue for himself.
